11: fn stack_ancestors(
12:     state: &StackState,
13:     branch: &str,
14:     repo: &str,
15: ) -> Vec<crate::stack_body::AncestorPr> {
16:     let path = state.path_to_trunk(branch);
17:     let len = path.len();
18:     if len < 2 {
19:         return vec![];
20:     }
21: 
22:     path[1..len - 1]
23:         .iter()
24:         .rev()
25:         .map(|b| {
26:             let pr_number = state.branches.get(b).and_then(|m| m.pr_number);
27:             let pr_url = pr_number.and_then(|n| {
28:                 if repo.is_empty() {
29:                     None
30:                 } else {
31:                     Some(format!("https://github.com/{repo}/pull/{n}"))
32:                 }
33:             });
34:             crate::stack_body::AncestorPr {
35:                 branch: b.clone(),
36:                 pr_number,
37:                 pr_url,
38:             }
39:         })
40:         .collect()
41: }

43: #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
44: pub fn run(
45:     draft: bool,
46:     no_draft: bool,
47:     no_pr: bool,
48:     title: Option<&str>,
49:     body: Option<&str>,
50:     body_file: Option<&str>,
51:     base_override: Option<&str>,
52:     stack: bool,
53:     stage_all: bool,
54:     stage_all_files: bool,
55:     commit_message: Option<&str>,
56:     repo_override: Option<&str>,
57:     remote_override: Option<&str>,
58: ) -> Result<()> {
59:     if stack {
60:         return crate::cmd::submit::run(draft, no_draft, title, body, body_file, repo_override, remote_override);
61:     }
62: 
63:     if let Some(root) = git::current_linked_worktree_root()? {
64:         ui::linked_worktree_warning(&root);
65:     }
66: 
67:     let mut commit_scope_defined = false;
68:     let mut commit_scope_mode: Option<String> = None;
69:     let mut commit_out_of_scope_files: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
70: 
71:     // If -a or -m was provided, do the commit first.
72:     if stage_all || stage_all_files || commit_message.is_some() {
73:         if let Some(msg) = commit_message {
74:             let stage_mode = if stage_all_files {
75:                 Some(StageMode::All)
76:             } else if stage_all {
77:                 Some(StageMode::Tracked)
78:             } else {
79:                 None
80:             };
81:             let outcome = mutation_guard::commit_with_guard(msg, stage_mode, false, &[])?
82:                 .expect("commit_with_guard returns Some when --if-changed is false");
83:             commit_scope_defined = outcome.scope.scope_defined;
84:             commit_scope_mode = outcome.scope.scope_mode.clone();
85:             commit_out_of_scope_files = outcome.scope.out_of_scope_files.clone();
86:             let current = outcome.current;
87:             ui::info(&format!("Committed on `{current}`: {msg}"));
88: 
89:             if let Ok(stat) = git::show_stat_head() {
90:                 let stat = stat.trim();
91:                 if !stat.is_empty() {
92:                     eprintln!("{stat}");
93:                 }
94:             }
95: 
96:             // Restack children (same as ez commit).
97:             let state = StackState::load()?;
98:             if state.is_managed(&current) {
99:                 let children = state.children_of(&current);
100:                 if !children.is_empty() {
101:                     crate::cmd::restack::run(false)?;
102:                 }
103:             }
104:         }
105:     }
106: 
107:     let mut state = StackState::load()?;
108:     let current = git::current_branch()?;
109: 
110:     if state.is_trunk(&current) {
111:         bail!(EzError::OnTrunk);
112:     }
113: 
114:     if !state.is_managed(&current) {
115:         bail!(EzError::BranchNotInStack(current.clone()));
116:     }
117: 
118:     let remote = match remote_override {
119:         Some(r) => r.to_string(),
120:         None => state.effective_push_remote(&current),
121:     };
122: 
123:     // Resolve --no-pr: flag > config > false
124:     let skip_pr = no_pr || state.no_pr.unwrap_or(false);
125: 
126:     // Resolve draft: --draft/--no-draft flags > config > false
127:     let effective_draft = if no_draft {
128:         false
129:     } else if draft {
130:         true
131:     } else {
132:         state.draft.unwrap_or(false)
133:     };
134: 
135:     let resolved_body: Option<String> = match body_file {
136:         Some(path) => Some(github::body_from_file(path)?),
137:         None => body.map(|s| s.to_string()),
138:     };
139: 
140:     let parent = if let Some(b) = base_override {
141:         b.to_string()
142:     } else {
143:         state.get_branch(&current)?.parent.clone()
144:     };
145: 
146:     // Push the branch with force-with-lease.
147:     let sp = ui::spinner(&format!("Pushing `{current}`..."));
148:     git::fetch_branch(&remote, &current)?;
149:     git::push(&remote, &current, true)?;
150:     sp.finish_and_clear();
151:     ui::info(&format!("Pushed `{current}`"));
152: 
153:     // Store push_remote when --remote was explicitly passed on the CLI.
154:     if let Some(r) = remote_override {
155:         state.get_branch_mut(&current)?.push_remote = Some(r.to_string());
156:     }
157: 
158:     if skip_pr {
159:         state.save()?;
160:         ui::success(&format!("Pushed `{current}` (no PR)"));
161:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
162:             "cmd": "push",
163:             "branch": current,
164:             "no_pr": true,
165:             "scope_defined": commit_scope_defined,
166:             "scope_mode": commit_scope_mode,
167:             "out_of_scope_count": commit_out_of_scope_files.len(),
168:             "out_of_scope_files": commit_out_of_scope_files,
169:         }));
170:         return Ok(());
171:     }
172: 
173:     let body_explicitly_set = body.is_some() || body_file.is_some();
174: 
175:     // Create or update the PR.
176:     let had_pr_before = state
177:         .get_branch(&current)
178:         .ok()
179:         .and_then(|m| m.pr_number)
180:         .is_some();
181: 
182:     let pr_url = push_or_update_pr(
183:         &mut state,
184:         &current,
185:         &parent,
186:         effective_draft,
187:         title,
188:         resolved_body.as_deref(),
189:         body_explicitly_set,
190:         repo_override,
191:     )?;
192: 
193:     let pr_number = state.get_branch(&current).ok().and_then(|m| m.pr_number);
194: 
195:     state.save()?;
196: 
197:     // Update stack sections on contiguous sibling PRs.
198:     update_contiguous_stack_bodies(&state, &current);
199: 
200:     ui::success(&format!("PR: {pr_url}"));
201: 
202:     ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
203:         "cmd": "push",
204:         "branch": current,
205:         "pr_number": pr_number,
206:         "pr_url": pr_url,
207:         "created": !had_pr_before,
208:         "scope_defined": commit_scope_defined,
209:         "scope_mode": commit_scope_mode,
210:         "out_of_scope_count": commit_out_of_scope_files.len(),
211:         "out_of_scope_files": commit_out_of_scope_files,
212:     }));
213: 
214:     Ok(())
215: }

217: /// Push-or-update logic shared with the `submit` command.
218: ///
219: /// Repo resolution order: `repo_override` (CLI --repo) > `branch.pr_repo` (stored from first push) > `state.repo` (global config) > None.
220: /// When `--repo` is explicitly passed, the value is stored in `branch.pr_repo` so future pushes reuse it.
221: ///
222: /// Returns the PR URL.
223: pub fn push_or_update_pr(
224:     state: &mut StackState,
225:     branch: &str,
226:     parent: &str,
227:     draft: bool,
228:     title_override: Option<&str>,
229:     body_override: Option<&str>,
230:     body_explicitly_set: bool,
231:     repo_override: Option<&str>,
232: ) -> Result<String> {
233:     // Normalize repo shorthand (e.g. "fork" → "owner/repo" from remote URL).
234:     let resolved_override: Option<String> =
235:         repo_override.map(|s| github::resolve_repo_shorthand(s));
236:     // Resolve effective repo: CLI flag > stored per-branch > global config > None
237:     let effective_repo: Option<String> = resolved_override
238:         .clone()
239:         .or_else(|| state.effective_pr_repo(branch));
240: 
241:     // Resolve the push remote for cross-fork head prefix.
242:     let push_remote = state.effective_push_remote(branch);
243: 
244:     let existing_pr = github::get_pr_status_in_repo(branch, effective_repo.as_deref())?;
245: 
246:     let pr_url = match existing_pr {
247:         Some(pr) => {
248:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(pr.number);
249: 
250:             // Build the full stack tree (PR number already known for existing PRs).
251:             let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch);
252:             let stack_tree = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
253: 
254:             // Update PR base only when the stack parent is genuinely an ancestor of this branch.
255:             if pr.base != parent {
256:                 if git::is_ancestor(parent, branch) {
257:                     if let Err(e) = github::update_pr_base_in_repo(
258:                         pr.number,
259:                         parent,
260:                         effective_repo.as_deref(),
261:                     ) {
262:                         ui::warn(&format!(
263:                             "Push succeeded but PR #{} base could not be updated to `{parent}`: {e}",
264:                             pr.number
265:                         ));
266:                     } else {
267:                         ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} base to `{parent}`", pr.number));
268:                     }
269:                 } else {
270:                     ui::warn(&format!(
271:                         "PR #{} base not updated: `{parent}` is not an ancestor of `{branch}` \
272:                          (stack metadata may be stale — run `ez sync` or update manually)",
273:                         pr.number
274:                     ));
275:                 }
276:             }
277: 
278:             // Body update logic:
279:             // 1. If user explicitly passed --body/--body-file: build full ez body.
280:             // 2. If existing body has ez markers: regenerate all ez sections.
281:             // 3. If title override only: update title.
282:             if body_explicitly_set {
283:                 let raw_body = body_override.unwrap_or("Part of a stack managed by `ez`.");
284:                 let refs_section = extract_references_for_branch(state, branch, parent);
285:                 let body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
286:                     raw_body,
287:                     Some("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."),
288:                     refs_section.as_deref(),
289:                     &stack_tree,
290:                 );
291:                 if let Err(e) = github::edit_pr_in_repo(
292:                     pr.number,
293:                     title_override,
294:                     Some(&body),
295:                     effective_repo.as_deref(),
296:                 ) {
297:                     ui::warn(&format!(
298:                         "Push succeeded but PR #{} could not be updated: {e}",
299:                         pr.number
300:                     ));
301:                 } else {
302:                     ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{}", pr.number));
303:                 }
304:             } else {
305:                 // Fetch current body to check for ez markers.
306:                 let current_body = github::get_pr_body_in_repo(pr.number, effective_repo.as_deref())
307:                     .unwrap_or_default();
308:                 if crate::stack_body::has_ez_markers(&current_body) {
309:                     // Regenerate ez sections, preserving user content above markers.
310:                     let parsed = crate::stack_body::parse_ez_body(&current_body);
311:                     let refs_section = if parsed.has_references_markers {
312:                         extract_references_for_branch(state, branch, parent)
313:                     } else {
314:                         None // User removed references subsection — don't regenerate.
315:                     };
316:                     let summary = if parsed.has_summary_markers {
317:                         Some(parsed.summary.as_deref().unwrap_or("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."))
318:                     } else {
319:                         None // User removed summary subsection.
320:                     };
321:                     let body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
322:                         &parsed.user_body,
323:                         summary,
324:                         refs_section.as_deref(),
325:                         &stack_tree,
326:                     );
327:                     if body != current_body {
328:                         if let Err(e) = github::edit_pr_in_repo(
329:                             pr.number,
330:                             title_override,
331:                             Some(&body),
332:                             effective_repo.as_deref(),
333:                         ) {
334:                             ui::warn(&format!(
335:                                 "Push succeeded but PR #{} body could not be updated: {e}",
336:                                 pr.number
337:                             ));
338:                         } else {
339:                             ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} body", pr.number));
340:                         }
341:                     } else if title_override.is_some() {
342:                         if let Err(e) =
343:                             github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr.number, title_override, None, effective_repo.as_deref())
344:                         {
345:                             ui::warn(&format!(
346:                                 "Push succeeded but PR #{} title could not be updated: {e}",
347:                                 pr.number
348:                             ));
349:                         }
350:                     }
351:                 } else if title_override.is_some() {
352:                     // No ez markers — only update title if requested.
353:                     if let Err(e) =
354:                         github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr.number, title_override, None, effective_repo.as_deref())
355:                     {
356:                         ui::warn(&format!(
357:                             "Push succeeded but PR #{} title could not be updated: {e}",
358:                             pr.number
359:                         ));
360:                     } else {
361:                         ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} title", pr.number));
362:                     }
363:                 }
364:             }
365: 
366:             pr.url
367:         }
368:         None => {
369:             // Derive the PR title from the first commit on this branch.
370:             let range = format!("{parent}..{branch}");
371:             let commits = git::log_oneline(&range, 1)?;
372:             let derived_title = commits
373:                 .first()
374:                 .map(|(_, msg)| msg.clone())
375:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| branch.to_string());
376: 
377:             let title = title_override.unwrap_or(&derived_title);
378:             let user_body = body_override.unwrap_or("");
379: 
380:             // Extract references from commit messages if no explicit body.
381:             let refs_section = extract_references_for_branch(state, branch, parent);
382: 
383:             // Build initial body with stack tree (current branch won't have PR link yet).
384:             let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch);
385:             let stack_tree = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
386:             let body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
387:                 user_body,
388:                 Some("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."),
389:                 refs_section.as_deref(),
390:                 &stack_tree,
391:             );
392: 
393:             // Compute cross-fork --head value.
394:             let head = github::cross_fork_head(branch, &push_remote, effective_repo.as_deref());
395: 
396:             let pr = github::create_pr_in_repo(
397:                 title,
398:                 &body,
399:                 parent,
400:                 &head,
401:                 draft,
402:                 effective_repo.as_deref(),
403:             )?;
404:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(pr.number);
405:             // Only persist pr_repo when --repo was explicitly passed on the CLI.
406:             if let Some(ref r) = resolved_override {
407:                 state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_repo = Some(r.clone());
408:             }
409: 
410:             // Now rebuild the tree with the PR number and update the body.
411:             let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch);
412:             let new_stack_tree = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
413:             if new_stack_tree != stack_tree {
414:                 let updated_body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
415:                     user_body,
416:                     Some("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."),
417:                     refs_section.as_deref(),
418:                     &new_stack_tree,
419:                 );
420:                 let _ = github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr.number, None, Some(&updated_body), effective_repo.as_deref());
421:             }
422: 
423:             ui::info(&format!("Created PR #{}: {}", pr.number, pr.url));
424:             pr.url
425:         }
426:     };
427: 
428:     Ok(pr_url)
429: }
430: 
431: /// Extract references from commit messages on a branch.
432: fn extract_references_for_branch(
433:     state: &StackState,
434:     branch: &str,
435:     parent: &str,
436: ) -> Option<String> {
437:     let range = format!("{parent}..{branch}");
438:     let messages = git::log_full_messages(&range).unwrap_or_default();
439:     if messages.is_empty() {
440:         return None;
441:     }
442: 
443:     let extracted = crate::commit_refs::extract_refs_from_messages(&messages);
444:     if extracted.is_empty() {
445:         return None;
446:     }
447: 
448:     // Build stack PR number map for resolving plain #N references.
449:     let mut stack_prs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
450:     for (name, meta) in &state.branches {
451:         if let Some(num) = meta.pr_number {
452:             let url = crate::stack_body::pr_url_for_branch_pub(state, name)
453:                 .unwrap_or_default();
454:             stack_prs.insert(num, (name.clone(), url));
455:         }
456:     }
457: 
458:     // Determine upstream repo for resolving plain #N refs.
459:     let upstream_repo = determine_upstream_repo(state, branch);
460: 
461:     let resolved = crate::commit_refs::resolve_refs(&extracted, &stack_prs, &upstream_repo);
462:     crate::commit_refs::format_references_section(&resolved)
463: }
464: 
465: /// Determine the upstream repo for resolving plain #N references.
466: ///
467: /// Priority: first parent's pr_repo → state.repo → default remote repo.
468: fn determine_upstream_repo(state: &StackState, branch: &str) -> String {
469:     // Walk up to find first ancestor with a pr_repo.
470:     let path = state.path_to_trunk(branch);
471:     for b in &path {
472:         if let Some(meta) = state.branches.get(b.as_str()) {
473:             if let Some(ref repo) = meta.pr_repo {
474:                 return repo.clone();
475:             }
476:         }
477:     }
478:     // Fall back to global repo config.
479:     if let Some(ref repo) = state.repo {
480:         return repo.clone();
481:     }
482:     // Fall back to `gh repo view` default.
483:     github::repo_name().unwrap_or_default()
484: }
485: 
486: /// Update the stack section on all contiguous PRs in the chain.
487: ///
488: /// After pushing a branch, this walks the contiguous PR chain and
489: /// re-renders the stack tree section on each sibling PR.
490: pub fn update_contiguous_stack_bodies(state: &StackState, pushed_branch: &str) {
491:     let chain = crate::stack_body::contiguous_pr_chain(state, pushed_branch);
492: 
493:     for branch_name in &chain {
494:         if branch_name == pushed_branch {
495:             continue; // Already updated during push.
496:         }
497:         let Some(meta) = state.branches.get(branch_name.as_str()) else {
498:             continue;
499:         };
500:         let Some(pr_number) = meta.pr_number else {
501:             continue;
502:         };
503: 
504:         let effective_repo = state.effective_pr_repo(branch_name);
505: 
506:         // Fetch current body.
507:         let current_body = match github::get_pr_body_in_repo(pr_number, effective_repo.as_deref()) {
508:             Ok(b) => b,
509:             Err(_) => continue,
510:         };
511: 
512:         if !crate::stack_body::has_ez_markers(&current_body) {
513:             continue; // No ez markers — skip.
514:         }
515: 
516:         // Build the tree from this branch's perspective.
517:         let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch_name);
518:         let new_stack = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
519: 
520:         // Replace only the stack subsection.
521:         if let Some(updated_body) = crate::stack_body::update_stack_section_only(&current_body, &new_stack) {
522:             if updated_body != current_body {
523:                 if let Err(e) = github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr_number, None, Some(&updated_body), effective_repo.as_deref()) {
524:                     ui::warn(&format!(
525:                         "Could not update stack section on PR #{}: {e}",
526:                         pr_number
527:                     ));
528:                 } else {
529:                     ui::info(&format!("Updated stack section on PR #{}", pr_number));
530:                 }
531:             }
532:         }
533:     }
534: }

536: #[cfg(test)]
537: mod tests {
538:     use super::*;
539:     use crate::test_support::{CwdGuard, init_git_repo, take_env_lock};
540: 
541:     #[test]
542:     fn stack_ancestors_orders_trunk_closest_first_and_builds_urls() {
543:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
544:         state.add_branch("feat/a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
545:         state.add_branch("feat/b", "feat/a", "bbb", None, None);
546:         state.add_branch("feat/c", "feat/b", "ccc", None, None);
547:         state.get_branch_mut("feat/a").expect("a").pr_number = Some(10);
548:         state.get_branch_mut("feat/b").expect("b").pr_number = Some(20);
549: 
550:         let ancestors = stack_ancestors(&state, "feat/c", "org/repo");
551:         assert_eq!(ancestors.len(), 2);
552:         assert_eq!(ancestors[0].branch, "feat/a");
553:         assert_eq!(ancestors[1].branch, "feat/b");
554:         assert_eq!(
555:             ancestors[0].pr_url.as_deref(),
556:             Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/10")
557:         );
558:         assert_eq!(
559:             ancestors[1].pr_url.as_deref(),
560:             Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/20")
561:         );
562:     }

571:         let ancestors = stack_ancestors(&state, "feat/b", "");

585:     #[test]
586:     fn draft_resolution_no_draft_flag_wins() {
587:         // --no-draft should override both --draft and config
588:         let no_draft = true;
589:         let draft = true;
590:         let config_draft = Some(true);
591: 
592:         let effective = if no_draft {
593:             false
594:         } else if draft {
595:             true
596:         } else {
597:             config_draft.unwrap_or(false)
598:         };

<system-reminder>
Instructions from: /Users/drewry.pope/ez-stack/.worktrees/feat-local-merge/AGENTS.md
# AGENTS.md

Instructions for AI agents working with `ez-stack`.

## Purpose

`ez` is a CLI for managing stacked pull requests on GitHub. If a repository has `.git/ez/stack.json`, branch management and PR operations should go through `ez`, not raw `git checkout -b`, `git commit`, `git push`, or `gh pr create`.

## Install or update the tool

```bash
# First install
pip install ez-stack
ez setup --yes    # configures shell integration (PATH + worktree auto-cd)

# Update to latest (auto-detects install method)
ez update

# Check for updates without installing
ez update --check
```

Requirements:

- `git`
- `gh`, authenticated via `gh auth login`
- Rust/Cargo available to install `ez-stack`

## Install the skill

Install the ez-workflow skill into the current repo so all agents auto-discover it:

```bash
ez skill install
# Installs canonical skill to .agents/skills/ez-workflow/SKILL.md
# Symlinks agent-specific skill roots like .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/
# Commit the file so other agents in this repo use it too
```

This installs the workflow defined in [`SKILL.md`](./SKILL.md).

## Core rule

When `.git/ez/stack.json` exists, prefer these commands:

- Create branch: `ez create <name>`
- Commit specific files: `ez commit -m "msg" -- path1 path2`
- Commit all tracked changes: `ez commit -am "msg"`
- Commit tracked + untracked changes: `ez commit -Am "msg"`
- Commit patch-selected hunks: `git add -p` then `ez commit -m "msg"`
- Diff vs parent: `ez diff --stat` or `ez diff --name-only`
- Get parent branch: `ez parent`
- Push current branch and create/update PR: `ez push`
- Push the stack: `ez submit`
- Sync after trunk changes or merges: `ez sync` or `ez sync --autostash`
- Inspect state programmatically: `ez status --json` and `ez log --json`

Avoid these raw commands in an `ez`-managed repo:

- `git checkout -b ...` → `ez create`
- `git commit -m ...` → `ez commit -m`
- `git push` → `ez push`
- `gh pr create` → `ez push`
- `git diff main...HEAD` → `ez diff`

## Output format

Every command appends a status line to stderr: `[ok | 45ms]` or `[exit:3 | 120ms]`.
Use this to branch on exit status and learn command cost.

Discovery: `ez` (no args) lists all commands (exit 0). `ez <command> --help` shows full details.

## Good agent patterns

```bash
# Check whether the repo is managed by ez
test -f .git/ez/stack.json && echo "ez-managed"

# Create from a specific base branch
ez create feat/my-change --from main

# Preferred: stage specific files and commit in one step
ez commit -m "fix: update parser" -- src/parser.rs

# Bulk update: stage tracked files and commit in one step
ez commit -am "chore: update generated fixtures"

# Bulk update including new untracked files
ez commit -Am "feat: add generated fixtures and new docs"

# Partial hunk selection stays in git
git add -p
ez commit -m "fix: keep only intended hunks"

# Multi-paragraph commit message
ez commit -m "feat: add parser" -m "Implements recursive descent parsing."

# Commit only if there are staged changes
ez commit -m "chore: update" --if-changed

# Self-review before push
ez diff --stat

# Get parent branch name for scripting
ez parent

# Sync safely with a dirty working tree
ez sync --autostash

# Read current stack state as JSON
ez status --json
ez log --json
```

## Exit codes

- `0`: success
- `1`: unexpected error
- `2`: GitHub / `gh` error
- `3`: rebase conflict
- `4`: stale remote ref
- `5`: usage error
- `6`: unstaged changes

## References

- [`README.md`](./README.md): user-facing overview and command reference
- [`SKILL.md`](./SKILL.md): full skill instructions for skill-compatible agents
- [`CLAUDE.md`](./CLAUDE.md): project context and design principles

</system-reminder>
